title: lupinus angustifolius subsp. cryptanthus
intro: Lupinus angustifolius subsp. cryptanthus, also known as narrow-leaved lupin, is a subspecies of the annual or biennial flowering plant lupin that is native to Australia and New Zealand. The medicinal properties of Lupinus angustifolius subsp. cryptanthus include its use as a treatment for gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its high fiber and saponin content. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and aid in weight management. The herbal preparations of Lupinus angustifolius subsp. cryptanthus may include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
